Main Guide.Unnamed: 7: FIELD DESCRIPTION

This field contains the five-digit Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) numeric code assigned by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST).  This code uniquely identifies every county (or county equivalent) in the United States, including U.S. territories.  The state is identified by the first two positions and the county is identified by the last three positions.  The unique FIPS Code for each jurisdiction is provided within the individual county keying instructions.  A complete list of FIPS codes can be found in the FIPS Code table.
The State code is based on the USPS State abbreviations.  Refer to State Code table for standardized abbreviation list.
The county field contains the county name or equivalent such as a borough or an independent city.   A complete list of county names can be found in the FIPS Code table.
This field applies to Alaska recordings only.  Alaska is divided into 34 recording districts.  Refer to Recording District code table for valid codes.  See Alaska sample image for reference.
Every record is assigned a Data Class code.   Refer to Data Class Code table for list of all codes.
Currently, the default value is "P" (Primary) for all Zillow keyed records.  Refer to Record Type Code table for list of all codes.

Identifies the date the document was recorded at the County Recorder's office.  
Format:  MMDDYYYY.  

NOTE:  For 3rd party vendor foreclosure data, the reported date may represent a different value (ie. "Listed Date").

The Document Number assigned to the document by the County Recorder's office at time of recording.  Not all jurisdictions assign a document number but may use a book & page instead.  Formats vary, but are standardized at the county level based on recorder's stamp (format included within the county keying instructions).   Some counties assign both a Document Number and a Book & Page number.

The Book Number assigned to the document by the County Recorder at time of recording.  Not all jurisdictions assign a Book & Page number but may use a document number instead.  Formats vary, but are standardized at the county level based on the recorder's stamp (format included within the county keying instructions).   Some counties assign both a Document Number and a Book & Page number.

The Page Number assigned to the document by the County Recorder at time of recording.  Not all jurisdictions assign a Book & Page number but may use a document number instead.  Formats vary, but are standardized at the county level based on the recorder's stamp (format included within the county keying instructions).   Some counties assign both a Document Number and a Book & Page number.

Identifies if the recorded document is a Correction/Re-Recorded instrument.   As of 1st Qtr 2021, for offshore, field is flagged with "Y" if document is a Re-Recorded or Correction instrument.
Valid values:
C = Correction Document (as of 1st Qtr 2021, no longer active for offshore; only active for selected vendor data)

R = Re-Recorded Document (as of 1st Qtr 2021, no longer active for offshore; only active for selected vendor data)

Y = Yes  

Default is blank.

The Recording Date of the prior document is captured here, if provided.  This field pertains to Re-Recorded/Correction instruments as well as selected additional document types (where instructed), such as Fulfillment Deeds and Revocation/Cancellation Deeds.   Format:  MMDDYYYY.

The Document Date of the prior document is captured here.  This field pertains to Re-Recorded/Correction instruments as well as selected additional document types (where instructed), such as Fulfillment Deeds and Revocation/Cancellation Deeds.  NOTE:  This data element is only captured if a prior Recording Date is not provided.  Format:  MMDDYYYY.

The Document Number of the prior document is captured here, if provided.  This field pertains to Re-Recorded/Correction instruments as well as selected additional document types (where instructed), such as Fulfillment Deeds and Revocation/Cancellation Deeds.

The Book Number of the prior document is captured here, if provided.   This field pertains to Re-Recorded/Correction instruments as well as selected additional document types (where instructed), such as Fulfillment Deeds and Revocation/Cancellation Deeds.

The Page Number of the prior document is captured here, if provided.  This field pertains to Re-Recorded/Correction instruments as well as selected additional document types (where instructed), such as Fulfillment Deeds and Revocation/Cancellation Deeds.

Valid field for Hawaii and Minnesota counties only as these states have two recording systems (Abstract & Torrens).   As of 1st Qtr 2021, for offshore, field is flagged with "Y" if document is a dual system recorded instrument.

Most documents are recorded in one system only, but for documents recorded in both systems this field is coded as:  

A = Dual System recording information captured relates to Abstract system (in Hawaii also known as Regular system).  (As of 1st Qtr 2021, no longer active for offshore; only active for selected vendor data.)

T = Dual System recording information captured relates to Torrens system (in Hawaii also known as Land Court system).   (As of 1st Qtr 2021, no longer active for offshore; only active for selected vendor data.)

Y = Yes

Default is blank.

Valid field for Hawaii and Minnesota counties only.  For documents recorded in both systems, the Recording Date of the alternate system is captured here.  Refer to Dual System Recording Stnd Code field to determine the system related to data in this field.  
Format:  MMDDYYYY.

Field Inactivated for offshore, as of 1st Qtr 2021

Valid field for Hawaii and Minnesota counties only.  For documents recorded in both systems, the Document Number of the alternate system is captured here.  Refer to Dual System Recording Stnd Code field to determine the system related to data in this field.

Field Inactivated for offshore, as of 1st Qtr 2021

Valid field for Hawaii and Minnesota counties only.  For documents recorded in both systems, the Book Number of the alternate system is captured here.  Refer to Dual System Recording Stnd Code field to determine the system related to data in this field.  

NOTE:  This field will be blank since HI & MN use a Document Number only at this time.

Field Inactivated for offshore, as of 1st Qtr 2021

Valid field for Hawaii and Minnesota counties only.  For documents recorded in both systems, the Page Number of the alternate system is captured here.  Refer to Dual System Recording Stnd Code field to determine the system related to data in this field. 
 
NOTE:  This field will be blank since HI & MN use a Document Number only at this time.

Field applies to Hawaii and Minnesota counties, Torrens recordings only.   The Transfer Certificate of Title Number (TCT# - Hawaii) or Certificate of Title Number (CT# - Minnesota) is entered here.   Format varies, but is standardized at the county level (format included within the county keying instructions).  A Transfer Certificate of Title provides documentary evidence of the right of ownership.   

Field Inactivated for offshore, as of 1st Qtr 2021

Identifies the type of document, generally determined by the document header or cover page.  Refer to Document Type code table for valid codes.  If document type is not given in either the document header or body, default code DEED is used for deed transfer documents.    All loan documents default to MTGE.    

NOTE1:  Vesting and Entity descriptions are ignored in Document header when there are competing doc types and instead reported in the Vesting and Description code fields.  Example: "Joint Tenancy Warranty Deed" captured as "Warranty Deed" and Vesting coded as "JT" (document must also identify the buyers as joint tenants in the grant portion of the document.)
NOTE2:  Example for Corrected and Re-Recorded documents:  if document headed "Correction Warranty Deed", the Document Type is coded as "WRDE" and field ReRecordedCorrectionStndCode is coded as "C".

The date of the document is generally provided on all documents, typically  found in the opening paragraph.  If a date is also reported at the signature line, it is captured in the Signature Date field.  NOTE:  If only a month and year provided, then "01" is entered for the day.  The Document Date will be the same as or earlier than the Recording Date.  

NOTE:  If the Document Date is greater than the Recording Date, it will be entered into the Effective Date field.
 
Format:  MMDDYYYY.

The date the document was signed (executed) is entered into this field, even if the Signature Date is the same as the Document Date.   If only a month and year provided, then "01" is entered for the day.  NOTE:  If neither a Signature Date nor Document Date nor Effective Date is provided, then the Notary Date is keyed into this field.

NOTE:  If the Signature Date is greater than the Recording Date, it will be entered into the Effective Date field.

Format:  MMDDYYYY.

Occasionally, documents will report an Effective Date.  If provided, it is entered into this field, even if this date is the same as either the Document Date or Signature Date.  If only a month and year provided, then "01" is entered for the day.  Format:  MMDDYYYY.

First and Middle Name or initial of Seller (Grantor) is entered into this field as reported on the document, including punctuation.  For names exceeding three words, all but the last word is entered into this field.  Exceptions are known multiple part last names such as "de la Cruz", which is keyed completely into Last Name field.
Last Name of Seller (Grantor) is entered into this field as reported on the document, including punctuation.
The complete name of the Seller; field populated for selected counties only.

If the Seller is a non-individual such as a business or a trust, the complete name is entered here, including punctuation.  Common business name endings are abbreviated according to standard abbreviations when programmatically possible.  Refer to Business name suffix table for abbreviations.

The description of the Seller as reported on the recorded document. Refer to Description Code list for valid codes.   Note:  Effective in April 2015, only one Seller Description Code per party name is captured.

Field Inactived, March 2017 Keying Reduction.
Simple sequence number for all seller names keyed from document.  Number 1 indicates first name, number 2 second name, etc.

Simple sequence number for all seller name description codes keyed for given seller.  Number 1 indicates first description, number 2 second description, etc.   This sequencing is per Seller, not per record.   Note:  Effective in April 2015, only one Seller Description Code per party name is captured. 

Field Inactived, March 2017 Keying Reduction.
Field populated if Care-of Name/Address reported for the Seller.  Care-of indicator is excluded from output.   Field Inactive since inception.

House Number portion of the full street address.  Example:  1475 Hidden Canyon Rd, "1475" is entered here.  NOTE:  If House Number is a numeric range, then enter complete range into this field, example "33-39").  Field Inactived, March 2017 Keying Reduction.

If the House Number contains an extension, it is captured here.  Example:  if 1475 1/2, then "1/2", if 1475-B, then "B".  NOTE:  If house number is a numeric range ("33-39"), then enter entire range into House Number field above and do not populate this field.

Transfer Tax (aka Documentary Fee, Excise Tax) is a tax levied on the sale or transfer of real estate.  Not all jurisdictions impose a City Transfer Tax, as well as some jurisdictions withhold disclosure of transfer tax.  As a general instruction, the City (Municipal) Transfer Tax is captured only when it is reported as a Total Amount.

Transfer Tax (aka Documentary Fee, Excise Tax) is a tax levied on the sale or transfer of real estate.  Not all jurisdictions impose a County Transfer Tax, as well as some jurisdictions withhold disclosure of transfer tax.

Transfer Tax (aka Documentary Fee, Excise Tax) is a tax levied on the sale or transfer of real estate.  Not all jurisdictions impose a State Transfer Tax, as well as some jurisdictions withhold disclosure of transfer tax.

Field populated by either:  1)  Total Transfer Tax amount reported on the recorded document, or 2)  The individual Transfer Tax field values are added together and output here (if only a County or a State Tax, then this field is left blank).

This flag field identifies if the transfer is IntraFamily based on either:  1)   Language reported on the document or 2)  Through a programmatic routine comparing buyer/seller names and presence of sales price.  If determined to be IntraFamily, then this field is coded as "Y", otherwise the field will be blank.
Non-Zillow Keyed field.  Field definition to be provided at a later date.  Pertains to Hawaii only.

This field flags if the transfer is Exempt from Transfer Tax.  
If exempt, then flagged as "Y", otherwise field defaults to blank.

NOTE:  A transfer may be exempt from Transfer Tax but still have consideration, for example, property acquired by or from a government entity.

Field Inactived, March 2017 Keying Reduction.

An assessor's parcel number or APN is a number assigned to parcels of real property by the county tax assessor for purposes of identification and record-keeping.  The APN is a unique property identifier for the parcel; the name of the number varies by jurisdiction.  The standardized format(s) is provided for each jurisdiction within the county keying instructions.  Whenever possible, the number is keyed to format.   

If multiple APNs are encountered, each APN is captured through the 100th APN.  Refer to field APNIndicatorStndCode for additional information regarding this cap.  APN is not always reported on the recorded document.
The purpose of this field is to capture certain detail regarding the APN.  Refer to APN Indicator Code tab for list of valid codes.

Some jurisdictions generate a unique number for the parcel for property tax purposes.   If present, this number is captured here and keyed to a standardized format provided within the county keying instructions.  Note that for some counties, the Tax ID Number is more commonly encountered on the recorded document than the APN.  

Effective, 4th Qtr 2017, If multiple Tax ID numbers are reported, only first Tax ID# is captured, in all cases.
The purpose of this field is to capture certain detail regarding the Tax ID Number.  Refer to Tax ID Indicator Code tab for list of valid codes.
This field is populated programmatically from Assessor Parcel Number field; punctuation is removed and spaces suppressed to produce a single string number.  Leading zeroes are retained.

If a document reports an alternate parcel number, in addition to the APN and Tax ID Number, this number is captured here.  Effective 4th Qtr 2017, if more than one Alternate Parcel Number is reported, only first Alternate Parcel Number is captured, in all cases.

This field pertains to Hawaii condominium parcels only.  This is a four-digit number for the Condominium Property Regime ("CPR") or Horizontal Property Regime ("HPR").  For condo parcels, this number also appears in the last segment of the APN (instead of "0000").

Not consistently encountered on the recorded document. Refer to Property Use table for list of valid codes.   NOTE:  For loans reporting "Single Family" at bottom of mortgage form, if no other property use is indicated, then the default is to code as "SR".

Legal descriptions for platted, subdivided land may include a Lot(s), and if so, it is captured here.   Multiple lots are separated with either an ampersand, comma or a dash.
Example "1&2", "4&10"; "2, 6, 9" or "10-14".  If a portion of a lot is transferred, then "POR" is keyed in front of the lot value.  Example:  "portion of lot 5", keyed as "POR 5".  If mixture of full and partial lots, the full lots are keyed first followed by the portion lots.  Example:   "portion of lot 4 and all of lot 5 and portion of lot 8", keyed as "5, POR 4&8"; Lot 2 and portion of 4 and 6-8.  keyed as "2, POR 4, 6-8".

NOTE:  In some jurisdictions, a "Lot" may be referred to as a "Parcel" (i.e. "Parcel A").  

Field Inactivated for Mortgages for offshore keying, as of 1st Qtr 2021.  Note that programmatic routines populating field from segment of property identifier may continue.

Legal descriptions for platted, subdivided land may include a Block(s), and if so, it is captured here.   If multiple, sequential blocks are transferred in a range, then a dash is used.  Example "1-2", "10-12".  If multiple, sequential ranges are reported or multiple blocks not in sequence, then a comma and space is used to separate the ranges or individual blocks.  Example: "1, 4".

Section-Township-Range legal descriptions are based on a land division system known as the Public Land Survey System.  
The complete STR legal is populated here with the following requirements:
1) Each element is separated by one space
2) Abbreviations used:  S = Section, T=Township; R=Range
3) Each element will be ordered or placed in Section-Township-Range order
4) Quarter section information will precede the Section information
5) Meridian information will follow the Range element

EXAMPLES:  
if reported as "North 1/2 Southeast quarter of Section 24 Township 32 north Range 18 east in 6th Principal Meridian", enter as "POR S24 T32N R18E 6PM"  NOTE:  As part of March, 2017 keying reduction, detailed sectional information will be reported as "POR" to reflect a portion of section    
NOTE1:  If reference to "corner", abbreviated to "COR".  Example, "NW COR"; if reference to "Southwest Part", abbreviated to "SW POR" 
NOTE2:  If reference to "Quarter Township" ("Quarter Township 3" or "Third Quarter"), abbreviated to "QT".  Example "QT3"
